div.p: 4 bytes (32 bits) — Points to a Page Table

Base Address = 0x00C01000 (Page Table at 12MB) AVL = 0 G = 0 (not global) PS = 0 (4KB pages) A = 0 (not accessed) PCD = 0 (cacheable) PWT = 0 (write-back) U/S = 1 (user accessible) R/W = 1 (writable) P = 1 (present)

20 bits — Physical address of the
page table (4KB aligned)
Only bits 31-12 stored;
bits 11-0 are implicitly 0

3 bits — Available for
OS use (ignored by CPU)

1 bit — Prevents TLB
flush on CR3 reload
(if PGE enabled in CR4)

1 bit — Set by CPU
on first access to page
(software clears it)

1 bit
0 = page cacheable
1 = page not cached

1 bit
0 = Ring 0 only
1 = Ring 3 can access

1 bit
0 = page table not in
memory (page fault)
1 = page table present
